data.title: PHASE III TRIAL OF ORCHIECTOMY/LHRH ANALOG + FLUTAMIDE + SURAMIN + HYDROCORTISONE VS ORCHIECTOMY/LHRH ANALOG + FLUTAMIDE IN PATIENTS WITH METASTATIC PROSTATE CANCER
data.brief_summary:
RATIONALE: Hormone therapy may be an effective treatment for prostate cancer.

PURPOSE: Randomized phase III trial to evaluate the effectiveness of treatment with flutamide and suramin with or without hydrocortisone in men who have metastatic or recurrent prostate cancer.
data.eligibility_criteria: DISEASE CHARACTERISTICS: Histologically confirmed adenocarcinoma of the prostate that is stage D2 Measurable or evaluable disease required with at least one of the following: At least 7 bone lesions Visceral involvement No more than 50% replacement of liver by tumor No clinical suspicion of brain me
